atoms.semantic_claim: RPC async validation is explicitly a user-feedback layer that delegates consensus-relevant payload checks to shared validation so invalid async traffic receives descriptive errors without becoming the authority over builder or verifier behavior.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Short-Running Async SPC Trace, Admission And Validation Compatibility scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: The old RPC async validation user-feedback layer is not restored as an authority surface. Consensus-relevant checks remain in shared payload validation and block verification; active Ritual RPC in v2.2 is result/delivery submission.

atoms.semantic_claim: RPC executor registration checks map each async precompile to its required TEE capability, allowing sovereign and persistent agents to use HTTP or LLM executors while enforcing media, ZK, FHE, and HTTP capabilities.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Executor And Operator Surface Compatibility scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Old RPC executor registration/capability preflight is not active in this v2 slice. Executor capability checks that affect validity are enforced by builder/verifier shared validation, not RPC-only feedback.

atoms.semantic_claim: RPC wallet validation calls the same commitment wallet verifier as the builder and translates insufficient balance, lock duration, and model failures into user-facing async validation errors.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Admission And Validation Compatibility, Product Economics Compatibility scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Old RPC wallet-validation preflight is not active in this v2 slice. Wallet checks remain consensus-relevant in builder/verifier through ritual-async-fees.

atoms.semantic_claim: RPC two-phase deadline validation delegates deadline extraction and offset-vs-TTL checks to the shared async validation module instead of maintaining a separate deadline rule.
atoms.required_change: No code change in this v2 slice; behavior is intentionally inactive or out of scope.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Replay the smallest Long-Running Two-Phase Trace scenario that exercises this source-owned behavior and fail if the v2.2 port omits or changes it.
atoms.notes: Old RPC two-phase deadline preflight is not active in this v2 slice. Shared async payload validation and txpool delivery deadline checks remain the live authority.
